DEV-QUAL-001.rationale: Core server orchestration is still split across protocol, config, and runtime seams under active extraction.
DEV-QUAL-001.mitigation: Keep entries isolated in the allowlist, review on every PR touching these files, and forbid new unrelated growth.
DEV-QUAL-001.closure_criteria: Split each file below the hard limit and remove the corresponding allowlist paths.

DEV-QUAL-002.rationale: CUDA fragment blocks remain grouped while kernel-level decomposition and performance checks are prepared.
DEV-QUAL-002.mitigation: Constrain edits to kernel extraction work and keep fragment boundaries documented in review notes.
DEV-QUAL-002.closure_criteria: Replace fragment bundles with smaller responsibility-focused units and remove the allowlist paths.

DEV-QUAL-003.rationale: Client composition files still aggregate module wiring and UI state that are scheduled for per-feature extraction.
DEV-QUAL-003.mitigation: Review changes for feature isolation and keep UI/module edits tied to decomposition work.
DEV-QUAL-003.closure_criteria: Extract module composition responsibilities into smaller units and delete the allowlist paths.

DEV-QUAL-004.rationale: The canonical unit-test catalog remains consolidated in one manifest while the quality tooling still consumes a single cross-domain test-group index.
DEV-QUAL-004.mitigation: Restrict edits to catalog maintenance, keep the allowlist isolated to this single manifest, and plan a later split by verification domain once manifest loader support is expanded.
DEV-QUAL-004.closure_criteria: Split the test-group catalog into smaller domain manifests and remove the corresponding allowlist path.

DEV-QUAL-005.rationale: Large manifest and workflow files exceeding 300 lines (requirements.json, release-lane.yml) containing necessary complex CI and traceability logic.
DEV-QUAL-005.mitigation: Keep entries isolated in allowlist and review on every PR touching these files.
DEV-QUAL-005.closure_criteria: Split files below the hard limit when architectural support for manifest merging is available.

DEV-QUAL-006.rationale: Unit tests for protocol codec exceeded file size limits after expanding test coverage to meet requirements.
DEV-QUAL-006.mitigation: Keep entries isolated in allowlist and review on every PR touching these files. Plan to split the file by test functionality.
DEV-QUAL-006.closure_criteria: Split the test file into smaller domain-specific files below the limit and update evidence traceability.

DEV-QUAL-007.rationale: CUDA buffer management remains consolidated while the octree GPU path is being decomposed and the new compact layout is being stabilized.
DEV-QUAL-007.mitigation: Keep this file isolated in the allowlist and review all changes for allocator and scratch-buffer correctness.
DEV-QUAL-007.closure_criteria: Split the CUDA buffer fragment below the hard limit and remove the allowlist path.

DEV-QUAL-008.rationale: The compact linear octree GPU fragment is still grouped while traversal and packing logic are being refactored together.
DEV-QUAL-008.mitigation: Keep this file isolated in the allowlist and review all changes for traversal correctness and memory layout stability.
DEV-QUAL-008.closure_criteria: Split the linear octree GPU fragment below the hard limit and remove the allowlist path.

DEV-QUAL-009.rationale: The physics system header still aggregates the particle system interface while the underlying runtime contracts are being reshaped.
DEV-QUAL-009.mitigation: Keep this header isolated in the allowlist and review all interface changes for consistency with the implementation files.
DEV-QUAL-009.closure_criteria: Split the physics interface header below the hard limit and remove the allowlist path.

DEV-QUAL-010.rationale: Qt workspace runtime-control integration tests remain consolidated while the shared runtime spy is extracted into focused test support.
DEV-QUAL-010.mitigation: Keep the exception isolated to this integration test file and require follow-up split work before adding unrelated UI scenarios.
DEV-QUAL-010.closure_criteria: Extract shared runtime test support and split runtime-control scenarios below the strong size threshold.
